name: Advanced Stash Operations for Context Switching
description: Master git stash to save, organize, and restore uncommitted work when switching between tasks or branches.

steps.title: Stash current changes with a descriptive message
steps.command: git stash push -m "WIP: user profile validation logic"
steps.description: Save all tracked modifications and staged changes to the stash with a message for easy identification later.
steps.tip: Always add a message with -m. The default 'WIP on branch' message is not helpful when you have multiple stashes.

steps.title: Stash including untracked files
steps.command: git stash push -u -m "WIP: new config files"
steps.description: The -u flag includes untracked files in the stash. Without it, only modified tracked files are stashed.
steps.tip: Use -a instead of -u to also include ignored files, though this is rarely needed.

steps.title: List all stashes
steps.description: View all stashed entries with their index, branch, and message. Stashes are numbered starting at 0 for the most recent.

steps.title: Inspect a stash without applying it
steps.description: View the full diff of a specific stash entry. The -p flag shows the patch; without it you only see a file summary.

steps.title: Apply a specific stash and keep it in the list
steps.description: Apply the changes from stash@{2} to your working tree. The stash entry remains in the list for future use.
steps.tip: Use 'git stash pop' instead of 'apply' to apply and immediately remove the stash entry.

steps.title: Drop a stash or clear all stashes
steps.description: Remove a specific stash entry with drop, or remove all entries with clear.
steps.warning: Dropped stashes cannot be easily recovered. The reflog may help for a short time, but do not rely on it.

fullScript:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# git-stash-helper.sh — Interactive stash management
set -euo pipefail

ACTION="${1:-list}"

case "$ACTION" in
  save)
    MSG="${2:-WIP: $(date +%Y-%m-%d_%H:%M)}"
    echo "Stashing changes with message: $MSG"
    git stash push -u -m "$MSG"
    echo "Done."
    ;;
  list)
    echo "=== Stash entries ==="
    STASHES=$(git stash list)
    if [ -z "$STASHES" ]; then
      echo "No stashes found."
    else
      echo "$STASHES"
      echo ""
      echo "Use '$0 show <index>' to inspect a stash."
    fi
    ;;
  show)
    INDEX="${2:-0}"
    echo "=== stash@{$INDEX} ==="
    git stash show -p "stash@{$INDEX}"
    ;;
  apply)
    INDEX="${2:-0}"
    echo "Applying stash@{$INDEX}..."
    git stash apply "stash@{$INDEX}"
    echo "Applied. Stash entry preserved."
    ;;
  pop)
    INDEX="${2:-0}"
    echo "Popping stash@{$INDEX}..."
    git stash pop "stash@{$INDEX}"
    echo "Applied and removed from stash list."
    ;;
  drop)
    INDEX="${2:?Usage: $0 drop <index>}"
    echo "Dropping stash@{$INDEX}..."
    git stash drop "stash@{$INDEX}"
    echo "Dropped."
    ;;
  *)
    echo "Usage: $0 {save|list|show|apply|pop|drop} [args]"
    ;;
esac

faq.question: Does git stash save staged changes separately?
faq.answer: By default, stash saves both staged and unstaged changes and resets the working tree. Use 'git stash push --keep-index' to stash only unstaged changes while keeping staged changes in the index.

faq.question: Can I create a branch from a stash?
faq.answer: Yes. Use 'git stash branch <new-branch> stash@{0}' to create a new branch from the commit where the stash was created, apply the stash, and drop it. This is useful if the stash conflicts with current changes.

faq.question: What happens if applying a stash causes conflicts?
faq.answer: Git will apply what it can and mark conflicts in the affected files, just like a merge conflict. Resolve the conflicts manually, then stage the files. The stash entry is not dropped automatically when conflicts occur.
